title: MPLS Tunnel / LSP Failure
symptoms: MPLS TE tunnel transitions to down state
Traffic loss on circuits riding the affected LSP
RSVP path or reservation messages failing
steps: Check tunnel status: 'show mpls traffic-eng tunnels brief' — identify down tunnels and their path
Check the explicit path: 'show mpls traffic-eng tunnels <tunnel-id> detail' — see which hop is failing
Verify RSVP state on the failing hop: 'show rsvp session' on intermediate routers
Check if the issue is path computation: 'show mpls traffic-eng topology' for link availability
Verify OSPF TE extensions are working: 'show ip ospf database opaque-area' — TE LSAs must be present
Check if manual path is configured — if so, verify all hops are reachable
Try forcing the tunnel to re-signal: 'mpls traffic-eng reoptimize tunnel <id>'
Check BFD if enabled on the tunnel for fast detection
resolution: If a hop is down: fix the underlying interface issue (see RB-NET-003). If path computation fails: verify OSPF-TE is distributing TE attributes. If reoptimization needed: use 'mpls traffic-eng reoptimize' to find a new path. For persistent failure: configure a fallback IP path with 'ip route' as backup.
escalation: If affected tunnels carry Tier-1 customer SLA traffic and do not recover in 15 minutes, escalate to MPLS specialist and open Priority-1 incident.
